content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://2024modulescorecard.pvel.com/damp-heat/", "source_title": "Damp Heat Test | Kiwa PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard 2024", "source_type": "industry_report", "source_quality": 0.75, "overall_relevance": 0.68, "year": 2024, "authors": ["Kiwa PVEL"], "venue": "PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard 2024", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Only 69% of bills of materials tested degraded by less than 6% after damp heat testing, with the DH failure rate doubling compared to the 2023 Scorecard, and 15 manufacturers experiencing at least one failure during DH testing due to delamination, junction box failures, electrical insulation faults, and power loss.", "direct_quote": "Only 69% of BOMs tested degraded by 6%. 11% of BOMs experienced one or more failures during DH testing. The DH failure rate was double that reported in the 2023 Scorecard, with 15 manufacturers experiencing at least one failure during DH testing. These were due to delamination, junction box lids falling off, electrical insulation faults and power loss. See the Failures page for more.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.72, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PVEL", "Kiwa", "69%", "11%", "DH failure rate", "delamination", "IEC 61730", "15 manufacturers"]}, {"statement": "Post-DH2000 delamination was observed with bubbles encroaching from glass-glass laminate edges under the frame to bussing ribbon and cell surface, creating a safety risk and non-compliance with IEC 61730, with potential root causes including use of POE plus EVA encapsulant instead of POE plus POE, reduced encapsulant thickness, and reduced lamination time.", "direct_quote": "A recent example of post-DH2000 delamination is shown here, with bubbles encroaching from the glass//glass laminate edge (under the frame) to the bussing ribbon and onto the cell surface. This creates a safety risk by forming a potential path from the module's current carrying parts to the grounded frame, and is a non-compliance for IEC 61730. The potential root causes of this could be various cost cutting measures, including the use of POE + EVA encapsulant rather than more traditional POE + POE", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.75, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Regulatory", "entities": ["DH2000", "delamination", "IEC 61730", "POE", "EVA", "glass-glass laminate", "encapsulant thickness", "lamination time"]}, {"statement": "Median power degradation following damp heat testing was 1.3% for bills of materials produced in 2022 and 1.6% for those produced in 2023, similar to the 72% pass rate reported in the previous year Scorecard, with the number of outliers and failures increasing year over year.", "direct_quote": "This was similar to the 72% reported in last year's Scorecard, with a median degradation following DH of 1.3% for BOMs produced in 2022 and 1.6% for BOMs produced in 2023. Similar to TC, the number of outliers and failures has also increased. Percentage of results shown that are post-BO stabilization: 19% of 2018, 46% of 2019, 95% of 2022, 86% of 2021. All 2022 and 2023 results are post-DH2000 (pre-BO stabilization)", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.68, "confidence": 0.87, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["median degradation", "1.3%", "1.6%", "72%", "DH2000", "BO stabilization", "PVEL Scorecard"]}], "evidence_summary": "PVEL 2024 industry scorecard showing rising DH failure rates (doubled from 2023), with 11% of BOMs failing DH testing. Post-DH2000 delamination linked to cost-cutting encapsulant choices (POE+EVA vs POE+POE). Relevant to understanding industry-wide encapsulant adhesion failures under damp heat conditions."}]}
